description: In June 2021, the FDA granted accelerated approval to Biogen’s Aduhelm (aducanumab) for Alzheimer’s disease despite conflicting data from clinical trials. The decision ignited a massive public health firestorm regarding regulatory integrity and the validity of surrogate endpoints in neurodegenerative disease treatments.

key_events.description: FDA Advisory Committee votes unanimously against recommending approval.
Internal FDA documents reveal cozy meetings between regulators and Biogen staff.
Leading hospitals (Cleveland Clinic/Mt. Sinai) refuse to administer the drug.
Medicare announces severe restrictions on coverage for the drug.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling institutional trust erosion when a regulatory agency conflicts with its own scientific advisory board.
